I have an entire phase, it's phase number seven on just team building and execution, but what positions are needed to actually deliver. I felt also needed to go inside and deliver your things. So I'm going to give you very actionable data here on what you need to build a dream team if you will for delivery. Now there's two different types of delivery. There's your production unit and there's your execution unit. But here I'm going to talk to you about delivery lines overall with what I feel you need in a perfect digital agency. So my very first position I ever hired besides a salesperson was a graphic designer. I can't tell you enough that whether you're in source, meaning hire somebody in-house to do this, either the contractor or as a staff member, you're a graphic designer. Outsource it, it doesn't matter. Find a team of people, get it done. The second thing that you need either in this individual here or in the second person is the ability to execute click models pages. The second person that I find potentially necessary is a copywriter. I'm going to explain why. Copywriters can create content for you. They can write content for you. They can also, if they have a multi-hat, be a critical position to create copy for you on the landing pages that your graphic designer can create. So now right here you have a little graphic design team that can also write copy that can go out of their way to crush anything in the market when it comes to click-phone pages that they created. Ads that they created, emails that they created. You've got the nice little digital marketing game right here. Now if you want to expand that out a little further, I would tell you just for the sake of being able to sell more services, the third position that you should hire, and you're doing these sequentially by the way, is a social media manager or otherwise known as a social media expert or social media specialist, whatever you want to call that position. Your social media manager should have full capabilities to have a comprehension that is around all social networks. Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, YouTube, Instagram, your social media person needs to be very skilled in all positions. They're coming to their abilities to write content, get content from your graphic design team, be able to competently cut up videos, strategize on what needs done, and also report on analytics to be able to plug in analytics into those tools as they come in. And being said, my favorite position, as you guys know, and you guys have watched other content I've come out with, is the project manager role. This person here, I like to have at least these three people in because a team of three people is manageable for you. You don't necessarily need a project manager yet because you don't have somebody who is going to be, you know, this isn't going to be too overwhelming having three people. However, if you want to have a business where you outsource everything, the only position you need is a project manager. So if you outsource, hire a project manager only, if you want to have a team of people yourself, you want to have a graphic designer first, copywriters, social media manager, then a project manager, you know. Following that role, I would tell you you want to roll into a digital marketer. A digital marketer can be a good position. If they're also a copywriter, because they don't have copyrighting capabilities, it kind of hurts the position overall. Because the social media, I'm sorry, the digital marketer in my opinion, the digital marketer is capability. You can also copy, right, dictates their skill and the position overall. You can dictate their abilities to be marketing automation, different ad copy, your ability to have them as a copy, sort of landing page copy and different headlines. Overall, it improves the condition of their position consistently and really impacts the result that you can get in a business depending on that person, that skill set or not. So let's get graphic design, copywriter, social media manager, project manager, a digital marketer, what are the positions do you really need? Some people would say you need a web developer. I disagree. What I would say that you might want to add on past this point, just because your time is going to free it up to go out and sell it. I would tell you at the same time you're selling, you might as well bring in what I call sales and marketing manager. Now sales and marketing manager, actually nothing to do with marketing besides just marketing your business with a word of mouth. We're not a manager actually. Somebody who has a good title because they need to represent themselves well in the market because they have a lot of potential client communications that they make. So sales and marketing manager are people who communicate once again with potential leads. You duplicate your sales self into this person. Now you have a little sales team. You can ride these people out for how many people that you want to add into this specific program. That being said, after you add a sales and marketing manager in, you got this nice little group of people here. You can typically duplicate these positions as necessary in your business for whichever ones are driving you the most return on investment. In some cases, you might want to add additional people into the business depending on what you are selling in the market. Keep in mind you might need more graphic designers if you're selling more graphic design related services. You might need more social media managers to be in your SMA business. You might need more project managers if you're outsourcing a ton of work and you actually start getting more work through time. If you're like me, the balance of these positions is fairly critical. Now the additional positions that I like to have, the time that I've added on, the videographers, that's been a great ball. Also you can call these people photographers or editors of some kind because they can have that multi-hat being able to edit content and anything that comes to them from clients that are also necessary to have. You can also have people like animators or creators who also have that multi-hat position as graphic designers in your business. You can also just have editors who their whole job is just to edit content. The digital agencies have the ability to really hire any positions that are critical that they need at the time that all relate to an ROI once again based on something that they can sell in the market. Sometimes I like to have a PR person in-house or as a partner that's always great once again to have as somebody once again to put either red content in a post as a contributor or just having a contributor in-house who can handle things like your speaking engagements, different things like this. Anyway, the dream team for what kind of clients, I'm sorry, for what kind of positions you need to have in your business to deliver comes from this little dream team right here. If you manage to hire these individuals sequentially, these ones being a little bit of an additional ride, but you'll be in a good position in business for you to hire these kinds of people, these individuals are the individuals that are critical for delivering. I find that project managers are a little more critical in other roles in the operation just because they look over everybody. I personally have a COO in my business. The reason you don't see it ahead of me down here is because an executive isn't necessary to your business at different levels. It's more critical to your business the farther in that you are comparatively to where you might be in business now. I have a COO because they cover the weaknesses of the areas that I'm not strong. Being said, a project manager once again is the person who looks over all these other individuals. They're the person who as I am saying what to do and how to do it to this said project manager, they are then taking that and going to the graphic design team, going to the videographer, going to the web developer, going to the copywriter, going to the digital marketer and they're disseminating the information throughout my organization. As they're disseminating that information as the project manager throughout my organization, anything that comes up or needs to come up goes through her first in my case because this is a female in my organization. Then it may come up to me rather than all the people in these communication lines talking to me all the time bogging me down, I'm working on the business not in the business. This line right here, everything below it in my opinion is working in the business and even above this is on the business. This is where I also keep my executive just in case you guys ever do that on a COO or anybody like that choose to follow. Things like the digital marketing. Obviously you're going to need a digital marketer, you're going to need a graphic designer in order to create the ads for the digital marketer to put together. You might need a copywriter. A digital marketer doesn't know copy well in order to write the ad copy. You're going to need a copywriter in order to write all the headlines and all the copy in your landing pages. You're going to need a graphic designer in order to create the landing pages on click phones since they'll be an expert at that too and have that multi-hat position, which we'll talk more about in the team building section. You also need that graphic designer to obviously create the graphics for the landing page. You need a project manager to look over all that stuff as you're selling whatever you're selling in the market at any time.
